title: sideritis perfoliata subsp. athoa
intro: Sideritis perfoliata subsp. athoa, also known as the "Mountain tea of Greece," is a plant species that belongs to the genus Sideritis and is native to the mountainous regions of Greece. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and acting as an antimicrobial agent, and is often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and digestive problems. Sideritis perfoliata subsp. athoa is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infused in hot water, and consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
